perturbation.original_text: You must pay us a royalty fee ("Royalty") equal to six percent (6%) of your Gross Revenues. The Royalty is in consideration of your right to use the Proprietary Marks and the System in accordance with this Agreement, and not in exchange for any specific services we render. If by reason of state or other law, we are prohibited from receiving a percentage of certain components of Gross Revenues (including alcoholic-beverage sales), you must pay us an equivalent amount by increasing the Royalty percentage applied to Gross Revenues exclusive of the prohibited components.
perturbation.changed_text: You must pay us a service fee ("Royalty") equal to six percent (6%) of your Gross Revenues. The Royalty is in consideration of your right to use the Proprietary Marks and the System in accordance with this Agreement, and not in exchange for any specific services we render. If by reason of state or other law, we are prohibited from receiving a percentage of certain components of Gross Revenues (including alcoholic-beverage sales), you must pay us an equivalent amount by increasing the Royalty percentage applied to Gross Revenues exclusive of the prohibited components.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'royalty fee' is changed to 'service fee' without any further definition. This change introduces uncertainty as the text later still reffers to Royalty payments as 'Royalty'. It creates an in-text contradiction. The term `Royalty` might imply different legal obligations compared to `service fee` due to legal and tax treatments.This perturbation leads to in-text contradiction when assessing the obligation for other payments and the general use of the term royalty in the original document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Due Date for Payment. Your Royalty payments and Advertising Contributions are due by the first (1st) day after the end of each Period (or the next business day if the first day is a Sunday or federal holiday) (as of the Agreement Date, the due date is Monday of each week). You must pay all other amounts due to us as specified in this Agreement or, if no time is specified, such amounts are due upon receipt of an invoice from us.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'royalty fee' as 'service fee' but continues to use the term 'Royalty' throughout the document. This creates a direct contradiction in the contradicted text, which refers to 'Royalty payments' and 'Advertising Contributions'. The use of 'Royalty' in the contradicted text now becomes ambiguous, as it's unclear whether it refers to the redefined 'service fee' or retains its original meaning, leading to uncertainty about the payment obligations. The original text uses the term Royalty, whereas the changed text introduces the term 'service fee', while still referring to Royalty payments.

perturbation.original_text: We have the right, at any time during normal business hours: (i) to conduct inspections of the Franchised Business; (ii) to interview your employees, work crews, and customers; and (iii) to review your business records, including those maintained electronically or off premises. We can initiate these actions with or without prior notice to you. You must cooperate with such inspections by giving our representatives unrestricted access and rendering such assistance as our representatives may reasonably request. If we notify you of any deficiencies after the inspection, you must promptly take steps to correct them. If you fail to correct any deficiencies within a reasonable time, we have the right to correct such deficiencies and to invoice you for our expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: We have the right, at any time during normal business hours: (i) to conduct audits of the Franchised Business; (ii) to interview your employees, work crews, and customers; and (iii) to review your business records, including those maintained electronically or off premises. We can initiate these actions with or without prior notice to you. You must cooperate with such audits by giving our representatives unrestricted access and rendering such assistance as our representatives may reasonably request. If we notify you of any deficiencies after the inspection, you must promptly take steps to correct them. If you fail to correct any deficiencies within a reasonable time, we have the right to correct such deficiencies and to invoice you for our expenses.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'inspections' to 'audits' in Section 6.13 introduces an in-text contradiction. While the text uses the term 'audits' the later part of the same section talks about 'inspection'. Since 'inspection' and 'audit' have distinct legal and operational meanings, this change causes confusion. An inspection generally refers to a visual assessment, while an audit implies a more thorough, systematic review, often focused on financial records. The in-text contradiction lies in the fact that the paragraph refers to 'inspection' when talking about 'deficiencies'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We have the right, at any time during normal business hours: (i) to conduct audits of the Franchised Business; (ii) to interview your employees, work crews, and customers; and (iii) to review your business records, including those maintained electronically or off premises. We can initiate these actions with or without prior notice to you. You must cooperate with such audits by giving our representatives unrestricted access and rendering such assistance as our representatives may reasonably request. If we notify you of any deficiencies after the inspection, you must promptly take steps to correct them. If you fail to correct any deficiencies within a reasonable time, we have the right to correct such deficiencies and to invoice you for our expenses.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'inspections' to 'audits' in the first sentence creates a contradiction because the subsequent sentences still refer to 'inspection' when discussing the process of identifying and correcting deficiencies. This misaligned terminology introduces ambiguity and conflicting obligations, as 'inspection' and 'audit' have distinct meanings and implications, thus it falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradiction stems from the paragraph referring to 'inspection' when talking about 'deficiencies', while now using 'audits' to describe the process as a whole.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that all fixed dollar amounts set out in this Agreement are subject to adjustment, up or down, depending on changes in the Index. For the purpose of this Agreement, the term "Index" is agreed to mean the Consumer Price Index (1982-84=100: all items; CPI- U; all urban consumers) published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (or if the Index is no longer published, a successor index that we may reasonably specify in the Manuals or otherwise in writing). We have the right to decide whether or not to make adjustments to fixed dollar amounts set out in this Agreement and if we decide to invoke that right, we will make changes not more than once each year by sending you written notice of the change.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that all fixed dollar amounts set out in this Agreement are subject to adjustment, up or down, depending on changes in the Benchmark. For the purpose of this Agreement, the term "Index" is agreed to mean the Consumer Price Index (1982-84=100: all items; CPI- U; all urban consumers) published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (or if the Index is no longer published, a successor index that we may reasonably specify in the Manuals or otherwise in writing). We have the right to decide whether or not to make adjustments to fixed dollar amounts set out in this Agreement and if we decide to invoke that right, we will make changes not more than once each year by sending you written notice of the change.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Index' to 'Benchmark' creates confusion, since the definition remains tied to 'Index' as the Consumer Price Index. This creates uncertainty as to which standard will be applied for adjustments. The text then uses 'Index' later which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that all fixed dollar amounts set out in this Agreement are subject to adjustment, up or down, depending on changes in the Benchmark. For the purpose of this Agreement, the term "Index" is agreed to mean the Consumer Price Index (1982-84=100: all items; CPI- U; all urban consumers) published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (or if the Index is no longer published, a successor index that we may reasonably specify in the Manuals or otherwise in writing). We have the right to decide whether or not to make adjustments to fixed dollar amounts set out in this Agreement and if we decide to invoke that right, we will make changes not more than once each year by sending you written notice of the change.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Index' with 'Benchmark' in the first sentence. However, the definition provided still defines 'Index' as the Consumer Price Index. The contradicted text also contains this change. Because the adjustment is supposed to be based on the 'Benchmark', but the 'Index' is defined to be the Consumer Price Index, it creates a contradiction because the definition of 'Index' does not match what 'Benchmark' is supposed to represent. This creates an in-text contradiction.
